templates.template: Template Customizado - Server HP iLO SNMP
templates.name: Template Customizado - Server HP iLO SNMP
templates.description:
Template Server HP iLO

MIBs used:
CPQSINFO-MIB
CPQHLTH-MIB
CPQIDA-MIB

Template tooling version used: 0.36

items.name: System: Temperature status
items.description:
MIB: CPQHLTH-MIB
This value specifies the overall condition of the system's thermal environment.
This value will be one of the following:
other(1)  Temperature could not be determined.
ok(2)  The temperature sensor is within normal operating range.
degraded(3)  The temperature sensor is outside of normal operating range.
failed(4)  The temperature sensor detects a condition that could  permanently damage the system.

items.name: Hardware model name
items.description:
MIB: CPQSINFO-MIB
The machine product name.The name of the machine used in this system.

items.name: Hardware serial number
items.description:
MIB: CPQSINFO-MIB
The serial number of the physical system unit. The string will be empty if the system does not report the serial number function.

triggers.expression: (last(/Template Customizado - Server HP iLO SNMP/system.hw.serialnumber,#1)<>last(/Template Customizado - Server HP iLO SNMP/system.hw.serialnumber,#2))=1 and length(last(/Template Customizado - Server HP iLO SNMP/system.hw.serialnumber))>0
triggers.name: Device has been replaced (new serial number received)
triggers.description: Device serial number has changed. Ack to close

items.name: Overall system health status
items.description:
MIB: CPQHLTH-MIB
The overall condition. This object represents the overall status of the server information represented by this MIB.

triggers.name: System status is in critical state
triggers.description: Please check the device for errors

triggers.name: System status is in warning state
triggers.description: Please check the device for warnings

dependencies.name: System status is in critical state

discovery_rules.name: Array Controller Cache Discovery
discovery_rules.description: Scanning table of Array controllers: CPQIDA-MIB::cpqDaAccelTable

item_prototypes.name: #{#CACHE_CNTRL_INDEX}: Disk array cache controller battery status
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: CPQIDA-MIB
Cache Module Board Backup Power Status. This monitors the status of each backup power source on the board.
The backup power source can only recharge when the system has power applied. The type of backup power source used is indicated by cpqDaAccelBackupPowerSource.
The following values are valid:
Other (1)  Indicates that the instrument agent does not recognize  backup power status.  You may need to update your software.

Ok (2)  The backup power source is fully charged.

Recharging (3)  The array controller has one or more cache module backup power  sources that are recharging.
Cache module operations such as Battery/Flash Backed Write Cache, Expansion, Extension and Migration are temporarily suspended until the backup power source is fully charged.
Cache module operations will automatically resume  when charging is complete.

Failed (4)  The battery pack is below the sufficient voltage level and  has not recharged in 36 hours.
Your Cache Module board  needs to be serviced.

Degraded (5)  The battery is still operating, however, one of the batteries  in the pack has failed to recharge properly.
Your Cache  Module board should be serviced as soon as possible.

NotPresent (6)  A backup power source is not present on the cache module board. Some controllers do not have backup power sources.

Capacitor Failed (7)  The flash backed cache module capacitor is below the sufficient voltage level and has not recharged in 10 minutes.  Your Cache Module board needs to be serviced.

trigger_prototypes.expression: count(/Template Customizado - Server HP iLO SNMP/system.hw.diskarray.cache.battery.status[cpqDaAccelBattery.{#SNMPINDEX}],#1,"eq","{$DISK_ARRAY_CACHE_BATTERY_CRIT_STATUS:\"failed\"}")=1 or count(/Template Customizado - Server HP iLO SNMP/system.hw.diskarray.cache.battery.status[cpqDaAccelBattery.{#SNMPINDEX}],#1,"eq","{$DISK_ARRAY_CACHE_BATTERY_CRIT_STATUS:\"capacitorFailed\"}")=1
trigger_prototypes.name: #{#CACHE_CNTRL_INDEX}: Disk array cache controller battery is in critical state!
trigger_prototypes.description: Please check the device for faults

trigger_prototypes.expression: count(/Template Customizado - Server HP iLO SNMP/system.hw.diskarray.cache.battery.status[cpqDaAccelBattery.{#SNMPINDEX}],#1,"eq","{$DISK_ARRAY_CACHE_BATTERY_WARN_STATUS:\"degraded\"}")=1 or count(/Template Customizado - Server HP iLO SNMP/system.hw.diskarray.cache.battery.status[cpqDaAccelBattery.{#SNMPINDEX}],#1,"eq","{$DISK_ARRAY_CACHE_BATTERY_WARN_STATUS:\"notPresent\"}")=1
trigger_prototypes.name: #{#CACHE_CNTRL_INDEX}: Disk array cache controller battery is in warning state
trigger_prototypes.description: Please check the device for faults

dependencies.name: #{#CACHE_CNTRL_INDEX}: Disk array cache controller battery is in critical state!
dependencies.expression: count(/Template Customizado - Server HP iLO SNMP/system.hw.diskarray.cache.battery.status[cpqDaAccelBattery.{#SNMPINDEX}],#1,"eq","{$DISK_ARRAY_CACHE_BATTERY_CRIT_STATUS:\"failed\"}")=1 or count(/Template Customizado - Server HP iLO SNMP/system.hw.diskarray.cache.battery.status[cpqDaAccelBattery.{#SNMPINDEX}],#1,"eq","{$DISK_ARRAY_CACHE_BATTERY_CRIT_STATUS:\"capacitorFailed\"}")=1

item_prototypes.name: #{#CACHE_CNTRL_INDEX}: Disk array cache controller status
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: CPQIDA-MIB
Cache Module/Operations Status. This describes the status of the cache module and/or cache operations.
Note that for some controller models, a cache module board that physically attaches to the controller or chipset may not be an available option.

trigger_prototypes.name: #{#CACHE_CNTRL_INDEX}: Disk array cache controller is in critical state!
trigger_prototypes.description: Please check the device for faults

trigger_prototypes.expression: count(/Template Customizado - Server HP iLO SNMP/system.hw.diskarray.cache.status[cpqDaAccelStatus.{#SNMPINDEX}],#1,"eq","{$DISK_ARRAY_CACHE_WARN_STATUS:\"invalid\"}")=1 or count(/Template Customizado - Server HP iLO SNMP/system.hw.diskarray.cache.status[cpqDaAccelStatus.{#SNMPINDEX}],#1,"eq","{$DISK_ARRAY_CACHE_WARN_STATUS:\"cacheModDegradedFailsafeSpeed\"}")=1 or count(/Template Customizado - Server HP iLO SNMP/system.hw.diskarray.cache.status[cpqDaAccelStatus.{#SNMPINDEX}],#1,"eq","{$DISK_ARRAY_CACHE_WARN_STATUS:\"cacheReadCacheNotMapped\"}")=1 or count(/Template Customizado - Server HP iLO SNMP/system.hw.diskarray.cache.status[cpqDaAccelStatus.{#SNMPINDEX}],#1,"eq","{$DISK_ARRAY_CACHE_WARN_STATUS:\"cacheModFlashMemNotAttached\"}")=1
trigger_prototypes.name: #{#CACHE_CNTRL_INDEX}: Disk array cache controller is in warning state
trigger_prototypes.description: Please check the device for faults

dependencies.name: #{#CACHE_CNTRL_INDEX}: Disk array cache controller is in critical state!

trigger_prototypes.name: #{#CACHE_CNTRL_INDEX}: Disk array cache controller is not in optimal state
trigger_prototypes.description: Please check the device for faults

dependencies.name: #{#CACHE_CNTRL_INDEX}: Disk array cache controller is in critical state!
#{#CACHE_CNTRL_INDEX}: Disk array cache controller is in warning state
dependencies.expression: count(/Template Customizado - Server HP iLO SNMP/system.hw.diskarray.cache.status[cpqDaAccelStatus.{#SNMPINDEX}],#1,"eq","{$DISK_ARRAY_CACHE_WARN_STATUS:\"invalid\"}")=1 or count(/Template Customizado - Server HP iLO SNMP/system.hw.diskarray.cache.status[cpqDaAccelStatus.{#SNMPINDEX}],#1,"eq","{$DISK_ARRAY_CACHE_WARN_STATUS:\"cacheModDegradedFailsafeSpeed\"}")=1 or count(/Template Customizado - Server HP iLO SNMP/system.hw.diskarray.cache.status[cpqDaAccelStatus.{#SNMPINDEX}],#1,"eq","{$DISK_ARRAY_CACHE_WARN_STATUS:\"cacheReadCacheNotMapped\"}")=1 or count(/Template Customizado - Server HP iLO SNMP/system.hw.diskarray.cache.status[cpqDaAccelStatus.{#SNMPINDEX}],#1,"eq","{$DISK_ARRAY_CACHE_WARN_STATUS:\"cacheModFlashMemNotAttached\"}")=1

discovery_rules.name: Array Controller Discovery
discovery_rules.description: Scanning table of Array controllers: CPQIDA-MIB::cpqDaCntlrTable

item_prototypes.name: {#CNTLR_LOCATION}: Disk array controller model
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: CPQIDA-MIB
Array Controller Model. The type of controller card.

item_prototypes.name: {#CNTLR_LOCATION}: Disk array controller status
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: CPQIDA-MIB
This value represents the overall condition of this controller,
and any associated logical drives,physical drives, and array accelerators.

trigger_prototypes.name: {#CNTLR_LOCATION}: Disk array controller is in critical state
trigger_prototypes.description: Please check the device for faults

trigger_prototypes.name: {#CNTLR_LOCATION}: Disk array controller is in warning state
trigger_prototypes.description: Please check the device for faults

dependencies.name: {#CNTLR_LOCATION}: Disk array controller is in critical state

item_prototypes.name: Fan {#SNMPINDEX}: Fan status
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: CPQHLTH-MIB
The condition of the fan.
This value will be one of the following:
other(1)  Fan status detection is not supported by this system or driver.
ok(2)  The fan is operating properly.
degraded(2)  A redundant fan is not operating properly.
failed(4)  A non-redundant fan is not operating properly.

trigger_prototypes.name: Fan {#SNMPINDEX}: Fan is in critical state
trigger_prototypes.description: Please check the fan unit

trigger_prototypes.name: Fan {#SNMPINDEX}: Fan is in warning state
trigger_prototypes.description: Please check the fan unit

dependencies.name: Fan {#SNMPINDEX}: Fan is in critical state

discovery_rules.name: Physical Disk Discovery
discovery_rules.description: Scanning  table of physical drive entries CPQIDA-MIB::cpqDaPhyDrvTable.

item_prototypes.name: {#DISK_LOCATION}: Physical disk media type
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: CPQIDA-MIB
Drive Array Physical Drive Media Type.The following values are defined:
other(1)  The instrument agent is unable to determine the physical drive’s media type.
rotatingPlatters(2)  The physical drive media is composed of rotating platters.
solidState(3)  The physical drive media is composed of solid state electronics.

item_prototypes.name: {#DISK_LOCATION}: Physical disk model name
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: CPQIDA-MIB
Physical Drive Model.This is a text description of the physical drive.
The text that appears depends upon who manufactured the drive and the drive type.
If a drive fails, note the model to identify the type of drive necessary for replacement.
If a model number is not present, you may not have properly initialized the drive array to which the physical drive is attached for monitoring.

item_prototypes.name: {#DISK_LOCATION}: Physical disk serial number
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: CPQIDA-MIB
Physical Drive Serial Number.
This is the serial number assigned to the physical drive.
This value is based upon the serial number as returned by the SCSI inquiry command
but may have been modified due to space limitations.  This can be used for identification purposes.

trigger_prototypes.expression: (last(/Template Customizado - Server HP iLO SNMP/system.hw.physicaldisk.serialnumber[cpqDaPhyDrvSerialNum.{#SNMPINDEX}],#1)<>last(/Template Customizado - Server HP iLO SNMP/system.hw.physicaldisk.serialnumber[cpqDaPhyDrvSerialNum.{#SNMPINDEX}],#2))=1 and length(last(/Template Customizado - Server HP iLO SNMP/system.hw.physicaldisk.serialnumber[cpqDaPhyDrvSerialNum.{#SNMPINDEX}]))>0
trigger_prototypes.name: {#DISK_LOCATION}: Disk has been replaced (new serial number received)
trigger_prototypes.description: Disk serial number has changed. Ack to close

item_prototypes.name: {#DISK_LOCATION}: Disk size
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: CPQIDA-MIB
Physical Drive Size in MB.
This is the size of the physical drive in megabytes.
This value is calculated using the value 1,048,576 (2^20) as a megabyte.
Drive manufacturers sometimes use the number 1,000,000 as a megabyte when giving drive capacities so this value may differ
from the advertised size of a drive. This field is only applicable for controllers which support SCSI drives,
and therefore is not supported by the IDA or IDA-2 controllers. The field will contain 0xFFFFFFFF if the drive capacity cannot be calculated
or if the controller does not support SCSI drives.

item_prototypes.name: {#DISK_LOCATION}: Physical disk S.M.A.R.T. status
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: CPQIDA-MIB
Physical Drive S.M.A.R.T Status.The following values are defined:
other(1)  The agent is unable to determine if the status of S.M.A.R.T  predictive failure monitoring for this drive.
ok(2)  Indicates the drive is functioning properly.
replaceDrive(3)  Indicates that the drive has a S.M.A.R.T predictive failure  error and should be replaced.

trigger_prototypes.expression: count(/Template Customizado - Server HP iLO SNMP/system.hw.physicaldisk.smart_status[cpqDaPhyDrvSmartStatus.{#SNMPINDEX}],#1,"eq","{$DISK_SMART_FAIL_STATUS:\"replaceDrive\"}")=1 or count(/Template Customizado - Server HP iLO SNMP/system.hw.physicaldisk.smart_status[cpqDaPhyDrvSmartStatus.{#SNMPINDEX}],#1,"eq","{$DISK_SMART_FAIL_STATUS:\"replaceDriveSSDWearOut\"}")=1
trigger_prototypes.name: {#DISK_LOCATION}: Physical disk S.M.A.R.T. failed
trigger_prototypes.description: Disk probably requires replacement.

item_prototypes.name: {#DISK_LOCATION}: Physical disk status
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: CPQIDA-MIB
Physical Drive Status. This shows the status of the physical drive. The following values are valid for the physical drive status:
other (1)  Indicates that the instrument agent does not recognize  the drive.
You may need to upgrade your instrument agent  and/or driver software.
ok (2)  Indicates the drive is functioning properly.
failed (3)  Indicates that the drive is no longer operating and  should be replaced.
predictiveFailure(4)  Indicates that the drive has a predictive failure error and  should be replaced.

trigger_prototypes.name: {#DISK_LOCATION}: Physical disk failed
trigger_prototypes.description: Please check physical disk for warnings or errors

trigger_prototypes.name: {#DISK_LOCATION}: Physical disk is in warning state
trigger_prototypes.description: Please check physical disk for warnings or errors

item_prototypes.name: Chassis {#CHASSIS_NUM}, bay {#BAY_NUM}: Power supply status
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: CPQHLTH-MIB
The condition of the power supply. This value will be one of the following:
other(1)  The status could not be determined or not present.
ok(2)  The power supply is operating normally.
degraded(3)  A temperature sensor, fan or other power supply component is  outside of normal operating range.
failed(4)  A power supply component detects a condition that could  permanently damage the system.

trigger_prototypes.name: Chassis {#CHASSIS_NUM}, bay {#BAY_NUM}: Power supply is in critical state
trigger_prototypes.description: Please check the power supply unit for errors

trigger_prototypes.name: Chassis {#CHASSIS_NUM}, bay {#BAY_NUM}: Power supply is in warning state
trigger_prototypes.description: Please check the power supply unit for errors

dependencies.name: Chassis {#CHASSIS_NUM}, bay {#BAY_NUM}: Power supply is in critical state

item_prototypes.name: Disk {#SNMPINDEX}({#DISK_NAME}): Layout type
item_prototypes.description:
Logical Drive Fault Tolerance.
This shows the fault tolerance mode of the logical drive.

item_prototypes.name: Disk {#SNMPINDEX}({#DISK_NAME}): Disk size
item_prototypes.description:
Logical Drive Size.
This is the size of the logical drive in megabytes.  This value
is calculated using the value 1,048,576 (2^20) as a megabyte.
Drive manufacturers sometimes use the number 1,000,000 as a
megabyte when giving drive capacities so this value may
differ from the advertised size of a drive.

item_prototypes.name: Disk {#SNMPINDEX}({#DISK_NAME}): Status
item_prototypes.description: Logical Drive Status.

trigger_prototypes.description: Please check virtual disk for warnings or errors
